replies.author: @lawlietriver8869
replies.text:
He killed fewer people in that show than Cap does in the mere opening of Winter Soldier. It is amazing to me that people pretend as if Steve Rogers didn't constantly murder people. The only difference is that Disney Marvel arbitrarily decided to put blood on the shield after Walker killed someone with it. For some mystical and magical reason, Steve killing someone does not produce blood. It would be so much better if they actually showed Walker doing something bad, rather than just have characters react as if he did something bad. Him taking the serum, somehow a bad thing despite it being entirely accepted that Steve took it. Him killing a guy with the shield, somehow bad despite it being perfectly accepted when Steve did it. The double standard is crazy and it makes no actual sense. He can only be bad if we also accept that Steve Rogers was bad. It is quite literally impossible to judge him as a bad/evil Cpt. America without also then calling Steve bad/evil. Since Steve, in Universe, is considered to be a fantastic guy, then it is shocking that someone that did far less bad things is somehow worse. Insanely, somehow, him killing 1 person is worse than Steve brutally murdering at least 6 in the span of 3 minutes. And the idea is not to shit on Stever Rogers, at all. The idea is to hold these people to the same standard. Within the standards that were put there for Steve, Walker is fine.

However, this was the same show that decided to show us terrorists murdering innocent people, and then have Falcon say that actually the evil thing is to call them terrorists. What a morally corrupt bunch of people that made that show. Killing an apartment buildings worth of innocent people = justified. Killing the person that 1 minute ago murdered your best friend, in cold blood = unforgivably evil. Shit makes no sense. People can use the weak excuse of "Well, Cap was stopping badguys and Walker did a revenge killing." His revenge killing stopped a super powered badguy, too. The word games are crazy. They both used lethal force to end the baddies. Either it is fine or it is not. But it can never be fine for one and not the other.

author: @sky_walker_1235
text:
Got me for a minute there Jeremy 🤣. I had low expectations for Thunderbolts, and despite not seeing any of the D+ shows, except for a few clips here and there, I really enjoyed it when I got out of the IMAX theater. I had a good time. It's not groundbreaking by any means but despite all of the comedy and shit, it's very different in tone, as it does tackle the points of mental health.

Overall, it is Yelena's movie, but Sentry?

Whenever Robert was in it, he stole the damn show.

And dare I say it, I liked the scenes between Yelena and Robert.

Bucky and everyone else were pretty great as well.

Looking back on it, you were right. It was the characters that drove this home despite the story.

Overall, I do give it a 3.75/5 Enjoyable, but might only watch it again cuz of Sentry and Yelena.
When it comes out on either online media.

Not gonna see F4 however. Split on seeing Doomsday when it does come out.
